info.title: SEMrush API
info.description: SEMrush Analytics API provides access to SEO and competitive intelligence data including domain analytics, keyword research, backlink analysis, and URL-level reports. The API uses a single endpoint with a 'type' parameter to specify the desired report.

get.description: Retrieve analytics data from SEMrush. The 'type' parameter determines which report is returned. Different report types require different parameters (e.g., domain reports require 'domain', keyword reports require 'phrase', backlink reports require 'target', URL reports require 'url'). Rate limit: max 10 requests per second per IP, max 10 simultaneous requests per user.

parameters.name: key
parameters.description: Your SEMrush API key. Obtainable from the Subscription Info page in your SEMrush account.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: The type of report to retrieve. Determines which data is returned and which additional parameters are required.

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description: The domain to analyze. Required for domain_* report types (e.g., 'example.com').

parameters.name: domains
parameters.description: Multiple domains to compare, separated by '|'. Used for domain comparison reports like domain_domains.

parameters.name: phrase
parameters.description: The keyword or phrase to analyze. Required for phrase_* report types.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to analyze. Required for url_* report types.

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: The target domain, root domain, or URL to analyze. Required for backlinks_* report types.

parameters.name: target_type
parameters.description: Specifies the type of the target for backlink reports.

parameters.name: database
parameters.description: The regional database to use (e.g., 'us', 'uk', 'de', 'fr', 'es', 'it', 'br', 'au', 'ca', 'ru', 'jp', 'in'). Defaults to 'us' if not specified. Required for domain and keyword reports.

parameters.name: export_columns
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of column codes to include in the response. Available columns vary by report type. Common codes include: Ph (Keyword), Po (Position), Nq (Search Volume), Cp (CPC), Ur (URL), Tr (Traffic), Co (Competition), Nr (Number of Results), Td (Trends), Dn (Domain), Rk (Rank), Or (Organic Keywords), Ot (Organic Traffic), Ad (AdWords Keywords), At (AdWords Traffic).

parameters.name: display_limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of results to return. Default varies by report type.

parameters.name: display_offset
parameters.description: Number of results to skip (for pagination).

parameters.name: display_sort
parameters.description: Sort order for results. Format is '{column_code}_{direction}' where direction is 'asc' or 'desc' (e.g., 'tr_desc' for traffic descending, 'nq_asc' for search volume ascending).

parameters.name: display_filter
parameters.description: Filter expression for results. Format: '{sign}|{field}|{operator}|{value}'. Sign: '+' (include) or '-' (exclude). Operators: Co (contains), Bw (begins with), Ew (ends with), Eq (equals), Gt (greater than), Lt (less than). Multiple filters joined with '|'. URL-encoded in requests.

parameters.name: display_date
parameters.description: Date for historical data in YYYYMM15 format (e.g., '20230115' for January 2023). Used with historical report types.

parameters.name: export_escape
parameters.description: Set to 1 to escape special characters in the response.

parameters.name: export_decode
parameters.description: Set to 1 to decode URL-encoded values in the response.

200.description: Successful response. Returns data in semicolon-separated values format (CSV with semicolons). Each line represents one data row. Column headers are not included by default; use the export_columns parameter to know the order of values.

400.description: Bad request. The request parameters are invalid or missing required fields.

403.description: Access denied. Invalid API key or insufficient permissions.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Maximum 10 requests per second per IP.

apiKey.name: key
apiKey.description: SEMrush API key. Obtain from your SEMrush account's Subscription Info page.
